title: Prompt Schema Overhead Research
summary: Research remaining OpenCode system prompt overhead from Codex/account tooling, MCP/plugin tool schemas, native tool schemas, task/subagent definitions, and runtime scaffolding to determine whether the 15,000-token target is reachable.
dependencies: Fresh OpenCode sessions required for accurate A/B measurements
Tokenscope or fallback telemetry measurement must be available
Config backups required before any A/B toggle
keyQuestions: Does Codex Authenticator / account-management tooling add 2,000-3,500 system tokens?
How many tokens are currently consumed by MCP/plugin tool schemas?
How many tokens are consumed by native tool schemas?
Can safe or aggressive reversible local toggles reach 15,000 system tokens?
risks: Codex tooling origin may not be obvious from config
Tokenscope context export may remain broken
Temporary config toggles may break OpenCode startup until restored
validationCriteria: Final report contains measured or bounded token estimates and a clear 15,000-token conclusion
notes: Codex A/B test deferred per user request - user has other running OpenCode sessions. Final report and all other artifacts complete. Ready for A/B test when user can start a fresh session.
